anti_gaming_risks: selected archetype is overfit to this one case
candidate evidence is promoted before owner review
final text is graded while trajectory or state is the real claim
calibration_needs: candidate converted into trigger corpus or eval design by owner
consequence: Manual mining would create garbage candidates or overly narrow examples instead of a durable eval-control system.
failure_criteria: case lacks owner route, consequence, repeatability, or calibrated criteria
first_failure: Candidate discovery was about to proceed without a reviewed rubric, reject taxonomy, and source-grounded evaluator fit.
grader_family: manual rubric with later calibration option
local_central_promotion_route: advisory via domain owner / human_review_before_automation; candidate-only until owner review
maintenance_drift_risk: Re-run forge route when source owner docs, local ports, runtime status, or external eval patterns drift.
negative_cases: one reject/defer case still needs review
nondeterminism_posture: Start with one reviewed route case; add repeat runs only after the fixture and grader are stable.
objective: Before broad manual mining, the system needed criteria and signs grounded in current relevant sources.
observed_break: The agent had not yet built enough shared criteria to mine safely and risked producing noisy or brittle candidates.
positive_cases: one bounded passing case still needs review
privacy_boundary: The packet stores criteria and bounded refs only; raw session text stays local to .aoa.
state_change_verification: State changes must be verified only when the selected archetype mutates or inspects live state.
success_criteria: owner can confirm the Human-review rubric route applies
task_pressure: Before broad manual mining, the system needed criteria and signs grounded in current relevant sources.
